resources.string: A dull bell tolls in the fog; polished armor remembers dust

The bell does not hurry. With each breath of fog, the wall sheds another pale scale; with each patient drop of acid rain, proud alloy reddens, crumbles, and forgets the shape it defended. Gears unclench inside their casings. Towers lean without protest. Rivers dry into a crust of old poison. Build higher if you wish. Time owns the ladder, the stone, and the hand that lifts them.
